title: roemeria refracta subsp. occidentalis
intro: Roemeria refracta subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western corn poppy or Western poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the family Papaveraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing antioxidant protection. Roemeria refracta subsp. occidentalis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
